Title: Akali Jhomen Tethi
Age: Current20 years old Timeline Born977 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent:
Akali was born to Master Mayym Jhomen Tethi, the Fist of Shadow, and her partner Tahno of the Kinkou Order. Whenever her parents were called away, other members of the Kinkou stepped in as Akali's surrogate family. She grew especially close to Master Kennen, the Heart of the Tempest, spending many hours learning his shuriken techniques. A precocious child, Akali absorbed knowledge like a sponge, and it soon became clear that she would one day follow in her parents' footsteps and help lead a new generation dedicated to preserving balance.

When Akali was nine, Master Kusho's adopted son and former student Zed returned to the Order after leaving to fight in the war against Noxus. He and his followers staged a bloody coup against the Kinkou, usurping their temple and slaying Master Kusho. While Akali and her mother managed to survive, her father Tahno did not.

Kusho's other son, Shen, returned from maintaining Ionia's spiritual harmony and led the remaining Kinkou to safety in the mountains. He took up his father's spirit blade and assumed the mantle of the Eye of Twilight, vowing to rebuild the Order. Following the core tenets, Shen began recruiting and training new members in hopes of restoring the Kinkou's strength. Kennen helped guide the Order as the new Eye of Twilight struggled to lead, and urged Master Mayym to begin considering Akali as her successor as the Fist of Shadow.

children.title: Rebuilding the Order
children.textContent:
Several months after the coup, the Kinkou retreated to an abandoned temple east of Thanjuul. On the last night of the Spirit Blossom Festival, her Mayym's apprentice, Faey, led the other neophytes on an unsanctioned hunt for a silver boar. Akali followed them despite being told to stay behind and her impatience allowed the boar to escape. Faey scolded her, causing Akali apologetically to offer her her plum for dinner.

When they returned to the temple, Mayym reprimanded them. While the other neophytes blamed Akali, Faey took responsibility for the hunt. Mayym punished them by having to go without dinner and dismissed them, but asked Faey to stay behind to talk further. Though Akali looked up to Faey, she had begun to feel neglected by her mother whenever she was present.

Later that night, Akali was practicing her calligraphy when Kennen returned to see her. He mentioned hearing about her role in disrupting the boar hunt, and Akali explained that she hadn't meant to interfere and wished that Faey had invited her. Kennen then asked whether Mayym had begun teaching her to use the kunai. When Akali said she had not, she confided her feelings of neglect, believing her mother favored Faey over her. Kennen explained that Mayy'’s attention to Faey stemmed from a promise she made after Faey lost her parents. Akali realized that the pain of losing her own father must have been doubled for Faey, and for far longer. Kennen reassured Akali of her potential and promised that her mother would see it one day. He encouraged her to finish her lesson, then departed to attend a meeting.

When Faey returned, Akali stayed up waiting for her and offered her a plum for dinner. Faey asked why she had it, reminding her that they had been punished with no dinner. Akali admitted that she had been saving food in case they ever had to flee from enemies again. Faey comforted her, then took a small bite of the plum before insisting that Akali eat the rest and for her to go to sleep.

Later, while playing in a nearby valley, Akali and the neophytes encountered members of the Navori Brotherhood. To avoid bloodshed, Faey devised a plan to frighten them away using the sound of grey owls, tasking Akali with warning the adults if necessary. The ruse succeeded, but the valley itself awakened and coalesced into a corrupted spirit, ensnaring the children in living vines. Faey managed to free some of the neophytes and ordered them to flee for help, but Akali stayed behind to distract the creature. When the spirit turned on Akali, Faey tried to save her, but she was caught in a rockslide, crushing her right leg and leaving her unconscious. Akali freed the other neophytes and tried to help Faey, when suddenly Kennen, Mayym, and Shen arrived and destroyed the spirit.

As Faey recovered, Akali and the other neophytes tried to comfort her. With her leg gone, Faey lashed out at Akali, berating her for not leaving when ordered. Confused and hurt, Akali left the hut. From the doorway, she watched as her mother comforted Faey. She left with tears in her eyes, unable to remember the last time her mother hugged her like that.

When Akali came of age at fourteen, she formally began her Kinkou training with Shen, determined to succeed her mother as the next Fist of Shadow. Though she lacked the magical abilities of many of her fellow acolytes, she was a prodigious fighter and quickly mastered the kama and kunai. However, Shen often found himself urging restraint when she faced mortal opponents.

children.title: Leaving the Kinkou Order
children.textContent:
After the Noxian Invasion ended, the Kinkou reached an uneasy accord with the Order of Shadow. Akali eventually proved that she was worthy and was named the new Fist of Shadow, allowing her mother to step down and focus on mentoring younger neophytes. However, Akali's spirit was restless. Though Ionia was now at peace, she saw that her homeland continued to suffer and began to question whether the Kinkou were truly fulfilling their purpose. As the Fist of Shadow, her duty was to Prune the Tree, but Shen would always urge restraint. Believing he was holding her back, Akali's youthful precociousness turned into open defiance. Before the entire Order, Akali denounced the Kinkou's inaction, declaring that their devotion to patience and spiritual balance had achieved little. She saw that Ionians were dying in the material realm and chose to protect them, leaving the Order. While Shen did not stop her, knowing this was a path Akali needed to walk alone, Kennen pleaded with her to stay.

Now living as an independent assassin, free from the restraints of the Kinkou's teachings, Akali lived as a vigilante, traversing the urban underground of Ionia and killing those that cause her people harm.

In the coastal village of Weh'le, a hidden phantom port with mystical properties, Akali went on a mission to assassinate a councilman from Puboe who had sold out his own people to Noxian invaders in exchange for gold and safe passage through. She visited her tattoo artist Bo'lii, a vastayan artist who ran a tavern in the port. While drawing a tattoo of a dragon on her back, the Puboe councilman walked into Bo'lii's tavern accompanied by three guards. Akali planned to kill them on the spot, but Bo'lii urged her to do her business outside of his tavern this time, to which she agreed. Once outside, Akali approached and killed the councilman's guards by hiding in a smoke cloud and firing several kunais before finally killing the councilman himself.

Despite leaving the Order, Akali still aided the Kinkou. She accompanied Shen to Navori Province, where they encountered a young Noxian woodsman cutting down a guardian tree. Intent on teaching him a lesson, Akali destroyed his cart, trapping him beneath it. As she confronted the boy, the tree's sap awakened its guardian, furious at the Noxian. Shen shared his ability to peer into the spirit realm with Akali, revealing the source of the guardian's rage. Despite his warnings, Akali rushed to attack the spirit, but was unable to stop it. Shen insisted that balance had to be restored, but Akali pleaded with him to bend the rules to spare the child. As Akali tried to free the boy, she realized what Shen meant and plucked a nearby seed. Using her kunai, she dug a small trench and instructed the boy to bury it. As the spirit prepared to strike, Akali used one of her charms to water the soil, causing the seed to bloom. Appeased, the guardian stayed its hand. Shen then nurtured the growing tree further, and the spirit settled beneath its roots, returning once more to its slumber.

children.title: Search for Zed
children.textContent:
In Zhyun, Akali got word that Zed recently assassinated the Kinkou master Althon, a cousin of Shen. Finding Shen in the Olgathee Forest killing corrupted spirits, Akali told him the news about master Althon, stating that she knows the town that Zed is staying in and suggesting she and Shen go confront him, which he reluctantly agrees to.

Once at a tavern in Thonx, the two found out from the innkeeper that Zed had already left and attacked a blossom festival before heading north to Shual. Akali suggested they follow him, but Shen stated that he had to travel south to slay demons that have been terrorizing a village. Akali tried to convince Shen to follow her to Shual, but Shen remained dedicated to his duties as the Eye of Twilight, urging his former student not to chase Zed alone. Akali, disregarding Shen's warning, took a boat and sailed down the quickest route to Shual.

Travelling to an inn near the Kinkou temple of Koeshin, Akali encountered a group of Zed's acolytes, including Zed's apprentice Shieda Kayn. She told a neophyte to head to the nearby Kinkou temple and gather reinforcements while confronted them. Akali asked where his master was, to which Kayn replied he was inside, likely killing Shen. He told her that was working for the wrong side of this conflict and offered her a chance to join, but Akali refused. Zed then exited the inn followed by Shen, who ordered Akali to let the Order of Shadow leave in peace since Zed declared the Code of Tuonn, an honorable call for temporary peace that allows them to let each other walk free without combat. Akali however wasn't satisfied with just letting Zed go and dashed in to kill him anyway, which Zed easily avoided by shadow shifting away while the rest of his acolytes quickly fled the Kinkou temple. Angered by Akali's recklessness, Shen told her never to fight Zed and that he'll fight him alone in the future, officially declaring that the armistice between the Order of Shadow and the Kinkou has ended.

Shen eventually revealed to Akali what transpired in the inn; Zed came to warn Shen that the serial killer Khada Jhin had escaped and that he seeks to bring him back to prison. Though Zed wanted to ally with Shen and capture Jhin, Shen refused and insisted on catching Jhin on his own without the help of the Order of Shadow, declaring that if he sees Zed interfere then he'll personally kill him and finally settle their scores.

children.title: Capturing Khada Jhin
children.textContent:
Akali followed Shen as he investigated a trail of intricate clues in search of the murderer Khada Jhin. Shen's investigation eventually lead him to believe that Jhin was working with the Kashuri faction, a radical nationalist group with ties to the Navori Brotherhood. This trail lead Shen to Nanthee, believing that the village elder was a potential target of Jhin's murder spree. Once at the scene of the crime, Shen stopped Akali from looking at the corpses, stating that she simply isn't ready to see the horrors that Jhin committed.

Eventually, Jhin's traps activated as dozens of bombs hidden in lanterns across Nanthee began exploding, killing many and causing massive damage to the city. Akali, noticing the explosion from afar, vowed to kill Jhin for what he'd done. Noticing a small ship in the distance and deducing that that's where Jhin was escaping, Akali demanded to know where it was headed, learning that it was headed to the Piltover.

Deciding to follow Jhin's trail alone, Akali set sail and eventually landed in Piltover. On her search for Jhin, Akali questioned and interrogated many of the locals, which lead her to the undercity of Zaun. In Zaun, she learned that Jhin had been attacking Piltover merchant clans that did business in exporting weapons to Ionia. By questioning the Piltover enforcers, Akali found herself at a theater in the Zaunite neighborhood of Mistfloor, where Jhin was said to have battled with the Ferros clan and their intelligencer agent.

At the theater, Jhin revealed an elaborate trap for Akali, wishing to make her the fourth victim in his disturbing art piece as three piltovans hung from Ionian trees strapped to machines. These machines began firing harpoons at Akali, who narrowly dodged them by tossing a smoke bomb before being hit unconscious by an explosive blast created by Jhin's rifle.

Jhin, having anticipated Zed and Shen's arrivals, set a trap by tying Akali up in a tree near a decoy of his. While Jhin tried to kill the two of them with Zaunite machinery, Zed managed to toss a shuriken at Akali, cutting her rope and freeing her from Jhin's trap. While Jhin was ready to fire a shot into Shen's head, Akali burst out of the smoke and punched Jhin to the ground, grabbing his gun and preparing to kill him for his crimes while Shen pleaded for her not to as he needed to bring Jhin to Ionia alive. Before she could kill him, Zed pushed her aside and grabbed Jhin, demanding he tell him how he got out of prison. Jhin simply replied with cryptic riddles, stating that Zed already knew who freed him before Zed threw Jhin to Shen and Akali's feet, stating that their bargain is done as he quickly fled Zaun.

children.title: The Second Noxian Invasion
children.textContent: At some point, Noxus began a second incursion of Ionia, sending massive armies accompanied by the undead juggernaut Sion. When the Ionian war hero Irelia began a resistance, she requested aid at a seaside village and received assistance from Kinkou members lead by Akali and Kennen, the spiritual leader Karma and the lone wanderer Yasuo. During the defense against the Noxian army, Akali targeted Sion and struck him with her kama and smoke bombs before joining Irelia and the rest of the champions in a final charge against the Noxians.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent:
Akali is a slender but muscular woman with light skin and red-brown eyes. Her thick black hair is often tussled, tied back in a large, messy bun which flares in every direction. She also has a tattoo of a pale blue dragon among white clouds swirling around her arms and across the backs of her shoulders.

Her typical professional attire is various shades of green with brown leather highlights. She wears baggy pants, a crop top, tabi, and a cloth mask that covers her lower face.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Akali is impatient, explosive and determined, preferring to leave the Kinkou Order rather than betray her own ideals and not fight for her people. It is notable that, despite this, she is still loyal to Ionia, being able to do anything, even to become a murderer, to protect the First Lands and her home.

children.title: Kinkou Order
children.textContent: Akali is currently a ninja gone rogue from the Kinkou Order. Although still part of it, she operates outside of its rules and regulations. She and Shen parted on neutral terms. Neither hates the other but both disapprove of the other's methods. While growing up in the Kinkou, Kennen trained her and helped her refine her shuriken techniques.

children.title: Zed
children.textContent: Zed might attempt to recruit her for his Order of Shadow. Akali; however, wants him dead since many Kinkou members including her father Tahno were killed by him and his order.

children.title: Kayn
children.textContent: Akali and Kayn know each other. He has tried to flirt with her multiple times and Akali finds him hot but too crazy for her taste.

children.title: Jhin
children.textContent: Akali temporarily teamed up with Shen to track down Jhin after they were informed of his escape. She later pursued Jhin to Piltover- Zaun and got captured by him, but was later saved with the help of Shen and Zed.

mainContent.textContent:
